customManagers.description: Track self-hosted (vendored) front-end assets via static/vendor/versions.txt; a PR here is a heads-up to re-vendor the dist files.

Track the OSV-Scanner binary version pinned in the scheduled scan. Renovate bumps OSV_VERSION; update OSV_SHA256 to match (the workflow's sha256sum -c fails loudly until you do — the hosted Renovate App can't recompute a binary checksum).

Track the explicitly pinned Alpine apk packages in the Dockerfile (each `ARG <PKG>_VERSION=x.y.z-rN` preceded by a `# renovate:` line) via repology, so every OS-package bump is a Renovate PR instead of silent drift.

Track the Chrome-for-Testing build the e2e leg pins (agent-browser fetches latest by default, which let Chrome 151 destabilize the 2-core leg — see #947). A bump is a Renovate PR that re-runs the e2e leg, so a new Chrome is verified green before it lands.

Track the pinned Shawl (Windows service wrapper) release in deps.py via github-releases (#934). Renovate bumps the `_*_VERSION` constant; the download URL derives from it in code, so version and URL can't drift. The `sha256` is NOT auto-updated (the hosted App reads github-releases' git-tag digest, not the release ASSET's file checksum), so refresh it from GitHub's published asset digest — `scripts/check_binary_dep_pins.py` verifies each pin against that digest and prints the correct value, and `deps install` fail-closes on a mismatch.

packageRules.description: atheris is pinned per-interpreter by hand in pyproject.toml — its wheel coverage shifts per release (3.1.0 dropped the cp311 wheel), so an auto-bump re-breaks the Python 3.11 CI leg (see #464 / #466). Renovate must not touch it. Remove this rule once Python 3.11 is dropped from the support matrix.

Do NOT float `.python-version`. It pins the CANONICAL dev interpreter (3.13 — the frozen binary + lint/build/coverage jobs all pin 3.13); it is not a dependency to bump. Renovate's `pyenv` manager (which tracks `.python-version`) bumping it to the newest CPython (#941: 3.13->3.14) silently overrode uv's interpreter — `uv run` in the lint job rebuilt the venv on 3.14 WITHOUT the dev extra, so ruff went missing and lint failed. Bump the canonical interpreter by hand, deliberately, like the CI matrix. (NB: the manager is `pyenv`, not `python-version` — the latter is a datasource, not a manager.)

js-yaml is pinned to v4 via an npm `overrides` entry in package.json — it is a transitive dep of markdownlint-cli2 (the dev-only `lint:md` tool), which does a default `import yaml from "js-yaml"`. js-yaml v5 is ESM-only and dropped the default export, so forcing v5 through the override breaks the required `lint` check (see #516). Cap Renovate below v5 until markdownlint-cli2 ships js-yaml v5 support; revisit then.
Vendored front-end assets require a manual dist re-vendor — never auto-merge; label for review.

Never auto-merge a Shawl (Windows service wrapper) bump (#934). Renovate updates the version + derived URL but CANNOT recompute the source-pinned sha256, so a human must refresh it from GitHub's published asset digest (the binary-dep-pins check prints the correct value) before merging. That check is intentionally NOT a required status check (required + path-filtered would strand unrelated PRs), so forcing a manual merge here is what guarantees the failed verification is actually seen and acted on.
Auto-merge patch + minor updates of dev/test tooling once CI passes (a failing check blocks the merge).

Auto-merge the Dockerfile's pinned Alpine apk packages (repology). The pinned-apk model 404s a build the moment Alpine publishes a newer -rN, so bumps must flow promptly rather than wait on a manual merge; the docker smoke-test + musl smoke-test in CI gate them, and a failing check blocks the merge. Grouped into one PR to keep the noise down. Scoped to the Dockerfile so a future repology-sourced dep elsewhere doesn't inherit blanket auto-merge.

Hold mkdocs-redirects at 1.2.2 — the last release published from the mkdocs GitHub org. 1.2.3 moved the project to a third-party org and added a hard dependency on `properdocs`, a second documentation engine that lands in the docs environment and prints promotional output into every build; it also caps `mkdocs<=1.6.1`, which would block future MkDocs upgrades. pyproject pins `==1.2.2`; this upper bound is what stops Renovate re-proposing the bump (it opened #997 before this rule existed). Revisit if upstream publishes a release without the `properdocs` dependency, or when the docs site migrates off MkDocs (#996). Security advisories are unaffected — vulnerabilityAlerts bypasses allowedVersions.

Hold Alpine base-image MINOR bumps (e.g. 3.24 -> 3.25) for manual review: a minor can move the system python3 to a new interpreter minor the CI support matrix must move with, so it must not auto-merge on a green smoke-test alone. Patch bumps (3.24.x) and digest re-pins still auto-merge via the base policy — only minor is held. Restores the pre-centralization clauster behavior (the old inline rule auto-merged Alpine patch only).
